_comment: Least-privilege permission policy for the AgentCore Runtime execution role (container deploy). Covers ECR image pull, CloudWatch Logs, X-Ray, CloudWatch metrics scoped to the 'bedrock-agentcore' namespace, bedrock:InvokeModel on a specific model ARN, and bedrock-agentcore:GetWorkloadAccessToken* on scoped workload identity resources. NOTE: do NOT include GetWorkloadAccessTokenForUserId in production when JWT is available - it accepts any opaque string without IdP verification. All resource wildcards in ECR/X-Ray that cannot be narrowed to a single ARN are noted inline. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-permissions.html

Statement._comment: GetAuthorizationToken does not support resource-level conditions; resource must be *.
DescribeLogGroups requires the broader log-group:* resource scope because it is a list-style call.
X-Ray PutTraceSegments/PutTelemetryRecords do not support resource-level conditions; resource must be *.
Scoped to the 'bedrock-agentcore' namespace via condition to prevent writing metrics to arbitrary namespaces.

GetWorkloadAccessTokenForJWT validates JWT signature, issuer, and expiry. Prefer over GetWorkloadAccessTokenForUserId in production. Resource scoped to the specific workload identity directory and agent name prefix.

Explicit deny for GetWorkloadAccessTokenForUserId: accepts any opaque string without IdP verification, enabling user impersonation. Remove this statement only in non-production environments where JWT is unavailable.

Scoped to the exact model ARN. Do NOT use arn:aws:bedrock:*::foundation-model/* - that grants invocation of any model, including expensive ones. Replace <MODEL_ARN> with the specific foundation-model or inference-profile ARN.
